beats.text: You stand at the foot of the mountains. A cold wind comes down through the pines.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: I should come up here more.
He will not come up here more.
The peaks stand indifferent. The minivan creaks in the parking lot, full of beer.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: This is exactly what Saturday is for. For... this.
A hawk circles. It is not impressed.
The mountains sway gently. You are not sure whether they or you are moving.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: If a tree falls in the forest and I'm real drunk — did it even fall?
Karen is calling. The phone is ringing inside the minivan. You cannot hear the phone. You can only hear the wind. And the wind is a phone.
